title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: The GoodMeet – Google Meet Integration for Webinar, Meeting & Video Conference plugin for...
description: The GoodMeet – Google Meet Integration for Webinar, Meeting & Video Conference plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Cross-Site Request Forgery in versions up to and including 1.1.8. This is due to a missing nonce verification in the reset_credential() function, which handles the wp_ajax_goodmeet_reset_google_meet_credential AJAX action. While the function does verify the user's capability (manage_options), it does not validate a nonce, making it susceptible to CSRF attacks. This makes it possib

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to perform unauthorized actions on behalf of an administrator by exploiting the lack of nonce verification in the reset_credential() function. This could lead to unauthorized changes to Google Meet credentials, potentially disrupting virtual meetings or exposing sensitive information.
recommended_actions: Update the GoodMeet plugin to a patched version (if available) or disable the plugin temporarily until a fix is released.
Implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious CSRF attempts targeting the vulnerable AJAX action.
Regularly audit and monitor administrative actions and logs to detect unauthorized changes to Google Meet credentials.

title: The Easy Upload Files During Checkout plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized access...
description: The Easy Upload Files During Checkout plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized access in all versions up to, and including, 3.0.1. This is due to missing authorization checks in the ufdc_custom_init() function, which processes the 'eufdc-delete' parameter without any nonce verification, capability check, or attachment ownership validation. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to permanently delete arbitrary media library attachments from the WordPress site.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6802.

title: The GW AI Website Builder plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized modification of data...
description: The GW AI Website Builder plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized modification of data due to a missing capability check on the gwaiwebu_gravitywrite_disconnect_handler() function in all versions up to, and including, 1.0.1. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Subscriber-level access and above, to disconnect the plugin from GravityWrite via the 'gwaiwebu_gravitywrite_disconnect' AJAX action.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated users with minimal access (Subscriber-level) to disconnect the GW AI Website Builder plugin from GravityWrite, potentially disrupting website functionality and causing data integrity issues.
recommended_actions: Update the GW AI Website Builder plugin to the latest version once a patch is released.
Restrict Subscriber-level access and review user roles and permissions to minimize exposure.
Monitor logs for suspicious activity related to the 'gwaiwebu_gravitywrite_disconnect' AJAX action.

title: The ICS Calendar plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Reflected Cross-Site Scripting via the ...
description: The ICS Calendar plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Reflected Cross-Site Scripting via the 'htmltagtitle' parameter in all versions up to, and including, 12.0.9 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that execute if they can successfully trick a user into performing an action such as clicking on a link. The vulnerability is reachable via the unauthenticated wp_ajax_nopriv_r34ics_

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-9838.

title: The Hostel plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'wphostel...
description: The Hostel plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'wphostel-book' shortcode in all versions up to and including 1.1.7. This is due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping on user-supplied shortcode attributes. Specifically, the second shortcode attribute (used as button text) is passed to the `$text` variable without sanitization at line 79 and then output directly into an HTML `value` attribute at line 91 without `esc_attr()` or any other escapi

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to inject malicious scripts into the 'wphostel-book' shortcode, which can execute in the context of users viewing the affected pages. This could lead to session hijacking, credential theft, or other malicious activities.
recommended_actions: Update the Hostel plugin to a patched version as soon as it becomes available.
Implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to block malicious input targeting the vulnerable shortcode.
Manually sanitize and validate user inputs in the shortcode attributes as a temporary mitigation measure.

title: The Eventin – Event Calendar, Event Registration, Tickets & Booking (AI Powered) plugin for...
description: The Eventin – Event Calendar, Event Registration, Tickets & Booking (AI Powered) plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'etn_faq_content' parameter in all versions up to, and including, 4.1.15 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers with contributor-level access and above to inject malicious scripts into pages, potentially leading to data theft, session hijacking, or defacement of affected websites. This could compromise user trust and lead to reputational damage for affected organizations.
recommended_actions: Update the Eventin plugin to the latest patched version once available to address the vulnerability.
Restrict contributor-level access to trusted users and review permissions to minimize exposure.
Implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious script injections.

title: The BetterDocs – AI Documentation, Knowledge Base, Docs, Wikis, FAQ with Chatbot plugin for...
description: The BetterDocs – AI Documentation, Knowledge Base, Docs, Wikis, FAQ with Chatbot plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to generic SQL Injection via the 'lang' parameter in all versions up to, and including, 4.6.0 due to insufficient escaping on the user supplied parameter and lack of sufficient preparation on the existing SQL query. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with custom-level access and above, to append additional SQL queries into already existing queries that can be used

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-15104.

description: The Cookie Banner for GDPR / CCPA – WPLP Cookie Consent plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to generic SQL Injection via the 'scan_id' parameter in all versions up to, and including, 4.3.6 due to insufficient escaping on the user supplied parameter and lack of sufficient preparation on the existing SQL query. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with administrator-level access and above, to append additional SQL queries into already existing queries that can be used to extract sens

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-14475.

title: The Import and export users and customers plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Sensitive...
description: The Import and export users and customers plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Sensitive Information Exposure in all versions up to, and including, 2.4.0 via the email_template_selected. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with subscriber-level access and above, to extract the post_title and raw post_content of arbitrary posts regardless of status (draft, private, future, trash, password-protected) or post type (including non-public CPTs such as WooCommerce orders and internal C

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-15026.

title: The FlowForms – Conversational Form Builder plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Insecure Direct...
description: The FlowForms – Conversational Form Builder plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Insecure Direct Object Reference in all versions up to, and including, 1.1.1 via the update_form due to missing validation on a user controlled key. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with contributor-level access and above, to modify the content, design, and settings of, as well as publish or revert, any form on the site — including forms owned by administrators — by supplying an arbitrary form ID

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers with contributor-level access or higher to modify, publish, or revert any form on the site, including those owned by administrators. This could lead to unauthorized changes, data manipulation, phishing attacks, or reputational damage.
recommended_actions: Update the FlowForms plugin to a patched version as soon as it becomes available.
Restrict contributor-level access to trusted users and review user roles and permissions.
Monitor form activity logs for any unauthorized changes and revert malicious modifications immediately.

title: The GDPR Cookie Consent plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized modification of data...
description: The GDPR Cookie Consent plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized modification of data due to a missing capability check and missing nonce verification on the gdpr_cookie_consent_ajax_save_schedule_scan() function (the wp_ajax_gcc_save_schedule_scan AJAX action) in versions up to, and including, 4.3.6. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Subscriber-level access and above, to modify the plugin's cookie scan schedule configuration stored in the gdpr_scan_schedule_data

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated users with minimal privileges (Subscriber-level access) to modify the cookie scan schedule configuration of the GDPR Cookie Consent plugin. This could potentially lead to tampering with compliance-related settings, disrupting website functionality, or enabling further exploitation.
recommended_actions: Update the GDPR Cookie Consent plugin to the latest patched version as soon as it becomes available.
Restrict user roles and permissions to minimize the risk of unauthorized access by limiting Subscriber-level accounts to trusted users only.
Monitor and audit plugin activity logs to detect any unauthorized changes to the plugin's configuration.

title: The Highlighting Code Block plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via...
description: The Highlighting Code Block plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via admin settings in all versions up to, and including, 2.2.0 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with administrator-level permissions and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page. This only affects multi-site installations and installations where unfiltered_htm

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-12108.

title: The Easy Appointments plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authorization bypass in all versions...
description: The Easy Appointments plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authorization bypass in all versions up to, and including, 3.12.27. This is due to the plugin not properly verifying that a user is authorized to perform an action. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with author-level access and above, to cancel all upcoming appointments site-wide by marking every future appointment stored by the plugin as abandoned. The nonce required to authenticate the cancellation request is printed

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-11992.

title: The Happyforms – Form Builder for WordPress: Drag & Drop Contact Forms, Surveys, Payments &...
description: The Happyforms – Form Builder for WordPress: Drag & Drop Contact Forms, Surveys, Payments & Multipurpose Forms plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Local File Inclusion in all versions up to, and including, 1.26.12 via the happyforms_get_form_partial() function. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Administrator-level access and above, to include and execute arbitrary .php files on the server, allowing the execution of any PHP code in those files. This can be used to bypass

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2025-11977.

title: Improper Privilege Management, Improper Access Control vulnerability in Apache IoTDB....
description:
Improper Privilege Management, Improper Access Control vulnerability in Apache IoTDB.
Authenticated users can escalate to full tree-path access by renaming
themselves to __internal_auditor.


This issue affects Apache IoTDB: from 2.0.8 before 2.0.10.

Users are recommended to upgrade to version 2.0.10, which fixes the issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows authenticated users to escalate their privileges, potentially gaining unauthorized access to sensitive data or control over the entire IoTDB tree-path structure. This could lead to data breaches, unauthorized modifications, or disruptions in IoT operations.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade Apache IoTDB to version 2.0.10 to patch the vulnerability.
Review and audit user accounts and access permissions to identify any unauthorized privilege escalations.
Implement monitoring and alerting for unusual activity, such as unexpected user renaming or access patterns.

title:
Uncontrolled Recursion, Uncontrolled Resource Consumption vulnerability in Apache IoTDB.
When...
description:
Uncontrolled Recursion, Uncontrolled Resource Consumption vulnerability in Apache IoTDB.
When pipe_air_gap_receiver_enabled=true, the IoTDB AirGap receiver's
readLength method calls itself recursively each time it recognises the
E-language prefix in socket data, with no depth limit. An unauthenticated
attacker can send a stream of repeated E-language prefixes that drives the
recursion arbitrarily deep, exhausting the receiver thread's JVM stack and
raising StackOverflowError.


This issue affect

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-40007.

title:
Out-of-bounds Read, Improper Input Validation vulnerability in Apache IoTDB C++ client.
Out-of...
description:
Out-of-bounds Read, Improper Input Validation vulnerability in Apache IoTDB C++ client.
Out-of-bounds reads in IoTDB C++ client TsBlock deserializer crash client
process on malformed server data.


This issue affects Apache IoTDB C++ client: from 1.3.5 before 1.3.8, from 2.0.5 before 2.0.10.

Users are recommended to upgrade to version 2.0.10, which fixes the issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-40454.

title:
Incorrect Authorization, Improper Access Control vulnerability in Apache IoTDB.
Authorization...
description:
Incorrect Authorization, Improper Access Control vulnerability in Apache IoTDB.
Authorization bypass in /rest/v2/fastLastQuery exposes last-value data to unauthorized authenticated users.


This issue affects Apache IoTDB: from 1.3.5 before 1.3.8, from 2.0.5 before 2.0.10.

Users are recommended to upgrade to version 2.0.10, which fixes the issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthorized authenticated users to access sensitive last-value data in Apache IoTDB, potentially leading to data leakage and unauthorized insights into critical IoT data.
recommended_actions: Upgrade Apache IoTDB to version 2.0.10 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Review and audit access control configurations to ensure proper authorization mechanisms are in place.
Monitor logs for any unauthorized access attempts to identify potential exploitation of the vulnerability.

title: Memory Allocation with Excessive Size Value, Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling...
description:
Memory Allocation with Excessive Size Value, Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling, Missing Authentication for Critical Function vulnerability in Apache IoTDB.
When pipe_air_gap_receiver_enabled=true, the IoTDB AirGap pipe receiver
accepts raw TCP connections on port 9780 with no authentication. The
readLength method reads an attacker-controlled 32-bit integer from the
socket and readData passes it directly to new byte[length] with no
upper-bound check. An unauthenticated attacker

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-40006.

title: Use of Externally-Controlled Input to Select Classes or Code ('Unsafe Reflection') vulnerability...
description:
Use of Externally-Controlled Input to Select Classes or Code ('Unsafe Reflection') vulnerability in Apache IoTDB.
The pipe processor reads a fully
qualified Java class name and
instantiates it using Class.forName().newInstance() without any
validation or allowlisting.


This issue affects Apache IoTDB: from 1.0.0 before 2.0.10.

Users are recommended to upgrade to version 2.0.10, which fixes the issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary code by exploiting unsafe reflection, potentially leading to remote code execution, data breaches, or system compromise. This poses a significant risk to systems relying on Apache IoTDB for IoT data management.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade Apache IoTDB to version 2.0.10 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Implement input validation and allowlisting for class names to prevent unsafe reflection.
Conduct a security audit to identify potential exploitation and ensure no unauthorized access has occurred.

title: CVE-2026-13722 - WatchGuard Fireware OS contains a firmware validation bypass when processing a backup image via the
description: WatchGuard Fireware OS contains a firmware validation bypass when processing a backup image via the backup/restore feature. An authenticated administrator can exploit this vulnerability to install a tampered firmware image.This vulnerability affects Fireware OS 11.0 up to and including 11.12.4_Update1, 12.0 up to and including 12.12 and 2025.1 up to and including 2025.6.2.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13722.

title: CVE-2026-13384 - An Out-of-bounds Write vulnerability in WatchGuard Fireware OS wgagent process could allow an authen
description: An Out-of-bounds Write vulnerability in WatchGuard Fireware OS wgagent process could allow an authenticated privileged user to execute arbitrary code via a specially crafted requests to the Management Web UI.This vulnerability affects Fireware OS 12.1 up to and including 12.12 and 2025.1 up to and including 2026.2.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows an authenticated privileged user to execute arbitrary code on affected devices, potentially leading to full system compromise, data breaches, or disruption of network operations.
recommended_actions: Immediately update WatchGuard Fireware OS to a patched version beyond 12.12 or 2026.2, as provided by the vendor.
Restrict access to the Management Web UI to trusted IPs and ensure strong authentication mechanisms are in place.
Monitor network traffic and logs for any unusual activity or exploitation attempts targeting this vulnerability.

title: CVE-2026-13377 - Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (XSS or 'Cross-site Scripting') vulnerab
description:

This issue affects Fireware OS 12.0 up to and including 12.12, 12.5 up to and including 12.5.18, and 2025.1 up to and including 2026.2.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13377.

description:

title: CVE-2026-13371 - An authenticated administrator can trigger a denial-of-service condition in the Fireware Management
description: An authenticated administrator can trigger a denial-of-service condition in the Fireware Management Web UI by sending malformed or crafted data to the put_data endpoint, which performs unsafe deserialization of the attacker-supplied input.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13371.

title: CVE-2026-13368 - WatchGuard Fireware OS contains a race condition leading to a use-after-free vulnerability in LDAP a
description:
WatchGuard Fireware OS contains a race condition leading to a use-after-free vulnerability in LDAP authentication for the Mobile User VPN with IKEv2. A remote unauthenticated attacker could exploit this vulnerability to execute arbitrary code in the context of the iked process on Fireboxes that have a Mobile VPN with IKEv2 configured to use an external LDAP authentication server.

This vulnerability affects Fireware OS 11.0 up to and including 11.12.4_Update1, 12.0 up to and including 12.12 and 2025.1 up to and including 2026.2.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13368.

title: CVE-2026-13084 - A null pointer dereference vulnerability in WatchGuard Fireware OS may allow a remote unauthenticate
description:
A null pointer dereference vulnerability in WatchGuard Fireware OS may allow a remote unauthenticated attacker to create a denial-of-service (DoS) condition by sending specially crafted IKEv2 messages. This vulnerability affects both the Mobile User VPN with IKEv2 and the Branch Office VPN using IKEv2 when configured with a dynamic gateway peer.

This vulnerability affects Fireware OS 11.10.2 up to and including 11.12.4_Update1, 12.0 up to and including 12.12 and 2025.1 up to and including 2026.2

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13084.

title: CVE-2026-13079 - A local privilege escalation vulnerability in the WatchGuard Mobile VPN with SSL client for Windows
description:
A local privilege escalation vulnerability in the WatchGuard Mobile VPN with SSL client for Windows allows a local attacker to escalate their privileges to NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM on the machine where the client is installed.

This issue affects the Mobile VPN with SSL client for Windows up to and including 2026.2.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13079.

This vulnerability affects Fireware OS 11.0 up to and including 11.12.4_Update1, 12.0 up to and including 12.12 and 2025.1 up to and including 2026.2.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13054.
